title: Smithsonian Starstruck VR exhibit lets you stroll through the stars
gist: Smithsonian Starstruck is a 40-minute location-based VR astronomy walk-through using HTC Vive Focus hardware, priced at $29-$35 solo, expanding to four US cities in 2026, with mixed technical quality but strong appeal for space enthusiasts.

insights.claim: Smithsonian Starstruck is a location-based VR experience priced at $29-$35 for solo adults and starting at $18 each for groups of four or more, with group discounts explicitly used to drive multi-person bookings.
insights.speaker: Ars Contributors
insights.so_what: Tiered pricing that rewards group size signals location-based VR operators leaning on social bundling to fill sessions and lift per-visit revenue.
insights.quote: solo adult tickets now ranging from $29 to $35 and group tickets for four or more starting at $18 each
insights.locator: Smithsonian Starstruck: An Immersive Experience is a 40-minute

insights.claim: The Starstruck VR exhibit debuted in Washington, DC in May 2026 and is scheduled to expand to Denver, Orlando, and San Antonio later in the year, indicating a multi-city rollout model for branded museum VR.
insights.speaker: Ars Contributors
insights.so_what: Multi-city expansion of a single VR title shows the touring/franchise playbook location-based entertainment is using to amortize content production costs.
insights.locator: It debuted in Washington, DC, in May

insights.claim: The DC exhibit runs on 2021-vintage HTC Vive Focus 3 headsets that visibly blur when the viewer moves their head, while other cities will use the newer HTC Vive Focus Vision, showing operators upgrading hardware mid-deployment.
insights.speaker: Ars Contributors
insights.so_what: Hardware refresh cadence in installed VR venues is a demand signal for HTC's enterprise headset line and a proxy for location-based VR margins.
insights.quote: the stars in my VR night were big and bright, but they blurred noticeably when I moved my head
insights.locator: The stars in my VR night were big and bright

insights.claim: The reviewer, a home Apple Vision Pro owner, judged the venue's older HTC hardware as not delivering enough 'technical wow,' highlighting a widening quality gap between premium consumer headsets and location-based commercial units.
insights.speaker: Ars Contributors
insights.so_what: As premium home VR improves, paid location-based VR must justify ticket prices on content and social experience, not hardware fidelity.
insights.quote: For a VR connoisseur with an Apple Vision Pro at home, the experience here might not deliver enough technical wow.
insights.locator: For a VR connoisseur with an Apple Vision Pro

insights.claim: Starstruck leans on NASA observatory brands (Parker Solar Probe, Hubble, Chandra, James Webb) and Smithsonian Astrophysical Observatory assets as licensed narrative content for the experience.
insights.speaker: Ars Contributors
insights.so_what: Public-science IP is being packaged into commercial immersive entertainment, a low-cost content moat for museum-branded VR.
insights.locator: Starstruck features three other of NASA's farthest-seeing observatories
